Remy Beauregard

Education

Dissertation: Essays on the Economics of Meaningful Work

BA in Economics & Psychology, Vassar College 2015–2019
Thesis: Conflict Exposure and Human Capital Development: Evidence from the Rwandan Genocide

Employment

Research Associate, International Group, Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco 2019–2021

Fields

Behavioral economics, experimental economics, development economics

Publications

  1. Remy Beauregard, Jens H.E. Christensen, Eric Fischer, Simon Zhu. “Inflation Expectations and Risk Premia in Emerging Bond Markets: Evidence From Mexico.” Journal of International Economics, 2024.

Working Papers

  2. What Moves Meaning? Investigating Drivers of an Idiosyncratic Incentive

Ongoing Projects

  1. Benefits on the Bench: Workfare, Mental Health, and the Role of the Team (with Carlos Brito)
  2. Do Social Expectations Discourage Graduates From Accepting Low-paid Jobs? Evidence From Ghana (with Grace Abban-Ampiah and Leïla Njee Bugha)
  3. pAIn or gAIn? Meaningful Work in the Age of AI
  4. Peer Effects on Novel Strategy Diffusion
